title: Review the x-auto task for the highest-risk failure modes when replacing stale unpublished drafts using current X trends and prior-post checks. Return terse bullets only.
summary_text: - [generate_draft_candidates.py:347-374](/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/scripts/generate_draft_candidates.py#L347-L374): “Current X trends” are only pulled from `trend_state.json`/quote seeds with no freshness gate, so the replacement can silently use stale trend context if the scanner is old or broken. - [trend_scanner.py:374-420](/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/trend_scanner.py#L374-L420): the scanner records `scanned_at`, but the generator never enforces it; fallback parsing also accepts model text when JSON parsing fails, which raises hallucination/staleness risk. - [generate_draft_candidates.py:139-208](/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/scripts/generate_draft_candidates.py#L139-L208): prior-post checks are handle-based only for quote candidates and are derived from current posts + posted history; there is no semantic dedupe against recent unpublished drafts, so near-duplicates can survive replacement. - [duplicate_guard.py:79-244](/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/duplicate_guard.py#L79-L244): duplicate protection exists for approved/posted queue state, but candidate generation bypasses it, so stale unpublished drafts are not blocked until much later in the pipeline. - [notion_sync.py:698-729](/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/notion_sync.py#L698-L729): candidate creation always writes a fresh draft row with schedule/publish fields stripped; there is no explicit update/match path for replacing an existing stale draft, so the old draft can remain live beside the new one.
